methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA signals intentional peer-to-peer knowledge distribution infrastructure in Richmond, moving beyond platform dependence toward community-driven AI fluency building that could bridge the metro-rural divide if scaled regionally.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia demonstrates distributed capacity-building in an economically transitioning region, though success depends on whether training produces deep AI capability or merely shallow platform adoption.

signals.title: NoVA School Systems Grapple with AI’s Role in the Classroom - Northern Virginia Magazine
signals.signal_reading: NoVA school systems wrestling with AI classroom integration reflects the region's proximity to cutting-edge technology adoption, but uneven policy responses across districts may fragment rather than distribute educational AI fluency even within the metro hub.

signals.title: Hampton Roads multiple listing service introduces AI tool to personalize home searches - The Virginian-Pilot
signals.signal_reading: Hampton Roads MLS deploying AI tools for home searches exemplifies shallow platform-delivered adoption—business efficiency gains without deep technical capability development, distributing AI consumption but not AI creation or understanding.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - MSN
signals.signal_reading: Young workers feeling AI pressure without corresponding skill-building infrastructure signals anxiety without empowerment, indicating neither deep capability development nor broad access to meaningful AI literacy is materializing at the pace of labor market disruption.
